lesson: c4-l02 / AI for Students with Visual Impairments
title: AI for Students with Visual Impairments

scenes.name: Title
scenes.text: When seeing the board isn't enough. [pause] AI that can read what a student cannot.

scenes.name: The barrier
scenes.text: Most teaching happens visually. [pause] The board. Slides. Worksheets. Diagrams. [pause] For a student with low vision, every one of these is a potential barrier — and waiting for help means missing the lesson.

scenes.name: Image mode
scenes.text: AI <emphasis>image analysis</emphasis> changes that. [pause] A student photographs the board. The AI reads the text, describes the diagram, explains the structure, and answers follow-up questions.

scenes.name: Tools
scenes.text: What the toolkit looks like. [pause] ChatGPT Vision mode for image analysis. ChatGPT Voice mode for spoken interaction. [pause] One device. Two channels. No special hardware.

scenes.name: Voice mode
scenes.text: Voice mode goes further. [pause] The student speaks a question. The AI speaks the answer. [pause] No reading. No typing. A conversation — like whispering to a classmate.

scenes.name: Scenarios
scenes.text: Try these in your classroom. [pause] Photograph the board after an explanation. Photograph a worksheet with small print. Photograph a lab setup the student cannot see clearly. [pause] Each becomes accessible content.

scenes.name: Limits
scenes.text: Limits matter. [pause] Image quality affects accuracy. Handwriting can be misread. Complex diagrams can be misinterpreted. [pause] Every description is an approximation — review it.

scenes.name: Your role
scenes.text: Your role is modest but essential. [pause] Allow phone use for AI support. Teach effective prompts. Set classroom norms for photography. [pause] And review the output.
